# API Checklist: Order Policy Confirmation

**Purpose**: Validate that the API-facing requirements for the order policy confirmation flow are complete, clear, consistent, and ready for implementation review.
**Created**: 2026-03-25
**Feature**: /Users/subho/sources/repos/friendly-octo-giggle/specs/003-order-policy-dialog/spec.md

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Do the requirements explicitly state whether any new endpoint, request field, or response field is introduced for the policy confirmation flow? [Completeness, Spec FR-001, Contract API Compatibility Contract]
- [ ] CHK002 Are the pre-submit and post-confirmation API boundaries fully specified so it is clear exactly when `POST /api/checkout` may be called? [Completeness, Spec FR-001, Spec FR-011, Spec FR-011a, Contract Interaction Contract]
- [ ] CHK003 Are authentication requirements for the delayed checkout submission path documented clearly enough to confirm that existing unauthenticated behavior remains unchanged? [Completeness, Contract API Compatibility Contract, Plan Constitution Check V]
- [ ] CHK004 Does the spec define whether any acknowledgment state must be sent to the backend or intentionally remain client-only? [Gap, Spec FR-011a, Contract Non-Goals]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK005 Is the statement that the checkout API "remains unchanged" precise enough to rule out hidden changes to payload shape, status codes, or polling semantics? [Clarity, Contract API Compatibility Contract]
- [ ] CHK006 Is the phrase "the only behavioral change is timing" specific enough to distinguish client-side interception from server-side validation or new backend confirmation logic? [Clarity, Contract API Compatibility Contract]
- [ ] CHK007 Are failure-handling requirements clear about what the client must do if policy content cannot be shown before any checkout request is made? [Clarity, Spec Edge Cases]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK008 Do the spec, plan, and contract consistently describe this feature as UI-only at the API boundary, with no new persistence and no change to order creation semantics after submission? [Consistency, Contract Scope, Contract Non-Goals, Plan Summary, Plan Technical Context]
- [ ] CHK009 Are the requirements consistent about the existing checkout flow continuing unchanged after confirmation, including enqueue and polling behavior? [Consistency, Plan Summary, Contract API Compatibility Contract]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK010 Are the non-submission scenarios fully covered, including dialog dismissal, unchecked acknowledgment, and policy-render failure, so it is unambiguous that no checkout API request should be sent in each case? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases, Contract Interaction Contract]
- [ ] CHK011 Are post-confirmation failure scenarios addressed well enough to determine whether existing API error responses, retry expectations, and user-visible recovery remain unchanged? [Gap, Contract API Compatibility Contract]
- [ ] CHK012 Are requirements defined for repeated checkout attempts so reviewers can verify whether multiple dialog opens still lead to the same unchanged API contract and policy version behavior? [Coverage, Spec FR-013, Contract API Compatibility Contract]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK013 Can the written success criteria objectively verify the API-facing promise that 100% of orders require review before submission, without needing undocumented instrumentation or inferred telemetry? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-001]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK014 Are the assumptions about existing checkout auth, enqueue, and polling services documented clearly enough that reviewers can tell which API behaviors are inherited rather than newly specified here? [Assumption, Plan Summary, Plan Technical Context]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
